description: Stripe webhook handler uses req.text() instead of raw Buffer; text() can alter unicode and invalidate HMAC signature verification. Found in production at synalux.ai 2026-05-16.

real_bugs.description: req.text() can re-encode unicode characters via WHATWG TextDecoder normalization. Stripe HMAC is byte-strict — any re-encoding invalidates signature. Must use Buffer.from(await req.arrayBuffer()).

process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET trimmed naively with `!` non-null. If env contains trailing newline/whitespace from copy-paste, signature verification fails. Also no support for test-mode + live-mode dual secrets.
real_bugs.fix_hint: Strip whitespace AND try both STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET and STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET_TEST in a loop

real_bugs.description: db.events.insert has no idempotency guard. Stripe retries on 5xx — second delivery of same event will succeed twice if not unique-constrained.
real_bugs.fix_hint: Use INSERT with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING or rely on Postgres unique constraint (code 23505) to detect dup

non_bugs: missing import of process (process.env is a Node global, always available)
missing return status code (NextResponse.json defaults to 200)

real_bugs_caught: medium-missing-await-on-db-insert (note: not actually a bug — the await IS present)
prism-coder:14b-v28.summary: Caught no real bugs; listed false positives despite explicit instruction

prism-coder:32b-v28.summary: Caught no real bugs; suggested defensive style not bugs

claude-sonnet-4-6.summary: Cloud frontier needed for the HMAC body-encoding gotcha
